title: allocarya tenuifolia
intro: Allocarya tenuifolia, also known as mountain fleabane, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. The plant is native to the mountainous regions of South America, where it grows in rocky and dry areas. Allocarya tenuifolia has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and infections. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and has been used to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
